questions.q: Who directed the 1974 film Ankur?
questions.explanation: Ankur (1974) was the directorial debut feature of Shyam Benegal.

questions.q: In which year was the film Ankur released?
questions.explanation: Ankur was released in 1974.

questions.q: Which actress made her feature-film debut in Shyam Benegal's Ankur?
questions.explanation: Ankur (1974) marked the feature-film debut of Shabana Azmi.

questions.q: The film Ankur is generally regarded as a landmark of which cinema movement in India?
questions.explanation: Ankur is considered a foundational film of the 1970s Indian Parallel cinema (New Wave) movement.

questions.q: What does the title 'Ankur' translate to in English?
questions.explanation: The Hindi word 'Ankur' means 'The Seedling' or 'sprout'.

questions.q: Which male actor made his screen debut as the landlord's son Surya in Ankur (1974)?
questions.explanation: Ankur launched the screen career of Anant Nag, who played Surya.

questions.q: In which Indian state is the rural story of Ankur (1974) primarily set?
questions.explanation: Ankur is set in rural Andhra Pradesh (in the Telangana region of the then-undivided state).

questions.q: What are the central social themes dramatised in Shyam Benegal's Ankur?
questions.explanation: Ankur dramatises caste oppression and feudal exploitation in rural India.

questions.q: Ankur (1974) was the first feature film directed by Shyam Benegal, who had earlier worked extensively in which field?
questions.explanation: Before Ankur, Shyam Benegal had a long career directing advertising and documentary films.

questions.q: Which character does Shabana Azmi play in the 1974 film Ankur?
questions.explanation: Shabana Azmi plays Lakshmi, the labourer woman at the centre of the story.

questions.q: How many National Film Awards did Shyam Benegal's Ankur (1974) win?
questions.explanation: Ankur won three National Film Awards: Second Best Feature Film, Best Actress (Shabana Azmi) and Best Actor (Sadhu Meher).

questions.q: For her performance in Ankur (1974), Shabana Azmi received which National Film Award?
questions.explanation: Shabana Azmi won the National Film Award for Best Actress for her role as Lakshmi in Ankur.

questions.q: In Ankur (1974), what is the central conflict between the landlord's son Surya and the labourer woman Lakshmi?
options: An exploitative relationship and an abandoned pregnancy
questions.explanation: Surya begins a relationship with Lakshmi and abandons her when she becomes pregnant, exposing feudal exploitation.

questions.q: Under which banner, linked to the advertising company Shyam Benegal worked with, was Ankur (1974) produced?
questions.explanation: Ankur was produced under Blaze Film Enterprises, the banner linked to the advertising firm Benegal worked with, with backing from Lalit M. Bijlani and Freni Variava.

questions.q: Ankur (1974) was filmed largely on location near which south-central Indian city?
questions.explanation: Ankur was shot on location in villages around Hyderabad in the Telangana region of the then-undivided Andhra Pradesh.

questions.q: Which actor played Kishtayya, Lakshmi's deaf-mute, alcoholic husband, in Shyam Benegal's Ankur (1974)?
questions.explanation: Sadhu Meher played Kishtayya, Lakshmi's deaf-mute alcoholic husband, winning the National Film Award for Best Actor for the role.

questions.q: Ankur (1974) is the first film in an informal rural-realist cycle by Shyam Benegal. Which of these films is also part of that cycle?
questions.explanation: Ankur was followed by Nishant (1975), Manthan (1976) and Bhumika (1977) in Benegal's early rural-realist cycle.

questions.q: At which major European film festival was Ankur (1974) nominated for the top prize, with Shabana Azmi noted for her performance?
questions.explanation: Ankur was nominated for the Golden Bear at the Berlin International Film Festival, bringing it international attention.

questions.q: Who served as the cinematographer on Shyam Benegal's Ankur (1974)?
questions.explanation: Govind Nihalani was the cinematographer on Ankur, beginning a long collaboration with Benegal.

questions.q: In which decade is Shyam Benegal's Ankur usually said to have opened the era of Indian Parallel cinema?
questions.explanation: Ankur (1974) is credited with opening the 1970s wave of Parallel cinema in India.

questions.q: Ankur (1974) was made primarily in which language?
questions.explanation: Ankur was made in Hindi, though set among Telugu-speaking rural characters.

questions.q: Which actor, later a leading figure of Parallel cinema, played the male lead Surya in Ankur (1974)?
questions.explanation: Anant Nag played the male lead Surya in Ankur, launching his film career.

questions.q: Shyam Benegal based the story of Ankur (1974) on a real incident from which region where he grew up?
questions.explanation: Benegal based Ankur on a true incident from the Telangana/Hyderabad region, where he grew up.

questions.q: Besides Best Actress for Shabana Azmi, the National Film Awards for Ankur (1974) also recognised which other performer?
options: Priya Tendulkar for Best Supporting Actress
Amrish Puri for Best Supporting Actor
questions.explanation: Sadhu Meher won the National Film Award for Best Actor for his role as Kishtayya in Ankur.

questions.q: The climactic final image of Ankur (1974) features which act by a village boy, symbolising future revolt?
options: Lighting a lamp in a temple
Hurling a stone at the landlord's house
Planting a tree in the field
Riding away on a bullock cart
questions.explanation: Ankur ends with a young boy throwing a stone that shatters a window of the landlord's house, the 'seedling' of rebellion.

questions.q: Which National Film Award did Ankur (1974) itself receive in the feature-film category?
questions.explanation: Ankur received the National Film Award for the Second Best Feature Film.

questions.q: Who composed the music for Shyam Benegal's Ankur (1974)?
questions.explanation: Vanraj Bhatia composed the music for Ankur, beginning a long association with Shyam Benegal's films.

questions.q: Shabana Azmi, cast in Ankur (1974), was a gold-medal graduate of which institution?
options: Film and Television Institute of India (FTII), Pune
Satyajit Ray Film and Television Institute, Kolkata
questions.explanation: Shabana Azmi was a gold-medal graduate in acting from the Film and Television Institute of India (FTII) in Pune.

questions.q: Which actress, later known for Marathi theatre and the TV serial Rajani, played Saru, the wife of Surya, in Ankur (1974)?
questions.explanation: Priya Tendulkar played Saru, the wife of the landlord's son Surya, in Ankur.

questions.q: Ankur's success helped Shyam Benegal go on to direct which 1976 film, famously funded by the contributions of dairy farmers?
questions.explanation: After Ankur, Benegal made Manthan (1976), financed by the small contributions of around 500,000 Gujarat dairy cooperative farmers.
